title: Exactly Which Characters Return from the Sea of LCL
display_title: Exactly Which Characters Return from the Sea of LCL
wikitext:
[]: Exactly Which Characters Return from the Sea of LCL?             Quote Post by Ceimoa Nan » Fri Mar 30, 2012 1:18 am Obviously, this has been discussed before, but I searched "alive instrumentality" and didn't see any threads devoted specifically to listing which characters join Shinji and Asuka in... existing. On the extremes, some people think Shinji and Asuka are the only ones to return, and others think everyone will return. But it seems to me the consensus is that while most people return from the Sea of LCL, there's a significant population without the will to live who won't return. I'd agree with that. Gendo, for example. And Seele. But then there're more questionable cases. Ritsuko seemed pretty depressed and self-loathing, and, well, tried to kill herself, but maybe she decided to live. Or Aoba? I bet most would say he had the will to live, but he was also, in Anno and Co.'s eyes, a nihilist who never got attached to anyone enough for Rei to take their form; maybe missing that spark of love is equivalent to not having the will to live. What about Fuyutsuki? And what about Kaji, who died a while before Instrumentality began, and might not have participated in Instrumentality in the first place? Hm--I just thought of this--what about Kyoko? So, I'd like it if you could list every character, and what you think their ultimate status is after EoE. Or maybe you just want to list particular characters you have a firm opinion on... I just want to see what the consensus is. Sure, you can't really base it on anything concrete, it's all character interpretation and fanwanking, but... yeah, exactly. Here's my input: SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by EvangelionFan » Fri Mar 30, 2012 5:57 am I agree with your list, with the exception of Gendo and Kyoko, who I would say are dead. In Gendo's case, he had his head biten off by Yui; I doubt Lilith-Rei would intervene in that. As for Kyoko, even if her soul (or soul segments) could have been retrieved by Lilith-Rei after the thrashing by the MP Evas, the fact of the matter is Unit-02 (Kyoko) was impaled by a replica lance of longinus - and in NGE, death by lance is more or less regarded as a finality. Additionally, we know that Yui left Earth in Unit-01/Ark - if she were to return it would not be from the ocean. Although Kaji was in EoTV Instrumentality, including him in EoE Instrumentality for that reason is heading into the murky waters of concurrency hypothesising.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Fri Mar 30, 2012 1:45 pm EvangelionFan wrote: and in NGE, death by lance is more or less regarded as a finality.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Fri Mar 30, 2012 2:18 pm I'd say that everyone who isn't dead will return as instrumentality seems to require all of humanity to work, and the more ppl leave, the less will be there to compliment the remaining ppl, tough I could imagine some Karmic justice striking when Keel manifests faaaaaaar away from all his fancy cybernetic life support. Personally, I think Gendo's final scene was just some particularly elaborated/dramatic tanging which we saw mostly from his own PoV (Yeah, we saw his lower body still standing there, but we saw other examples of tanging not happening all at once, like, Maya's han, that random soldier's arm...) For what it's worth, the unifinished EoE script has exactly the same dialogue there, but he simply goes slosdh, and there were other scenes that are the same except for more "exiting" visuals, for example, Asuka's reunion with Kyoko was originally just going to have that image of her smiling. Also, it wouldn't really fit the spirit of it all if the "You live in a world with a father that is ...let's say somewhat difficult to deal with"-problem was "solved" by that father conveniently dissapearing off the face of the earth.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Fri Mar 30, 2012 3:05 pm Everyone who existed inbetween 2nd and 3rd Impact probably has the oppurtunity to come back, including Kyoko. Yui became a Second Generation Seed of Life, and I imagine that Gendo, Kaworu, the Angels, and SEELE become the souls she uses to populate a new planet. Rei's spirit transcends time as Shinji's spirit guide. :3

[]:             Quote Post by esselfortium » Fri Mar 30, 2012 3:19 pm AuraTwilight wrote: Yui became a Second Generation Seed of Life, and I imagine that Gendo, Kaworu, the Angels, and SEELE become the souls she uses to populate a new planet.

[]:             Quote Post by EvangelionFan » Fri Mar 30, 2012 5:57 pm Mr. Tines wrote: Arael is there with all the rest in the angel tower.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Fri Mar 30, 2012 6:06 pm EvangelionFan wrote: There is no Rei-messenger in that shot. To me this choice rules out the possibility of Kyoko's soul surviving the battle and the 'feeding' of the Harpies.

[]:             Quote Post by Na7e » Fri Mar 30, 2012 6:08 pm EvangelionFan wrote: Remember this shot from '26 of Unit-02's mangled head lying alone on the ground? Now, consider all of the shots in which Rei-messengers are seen to be hovering over the bodies/LCL of dead NERV personnel/soldiers and interacting with the cast. There is no Rei-messenger in that shot. To me this choice rules out the possibility of Kyoko's soul surviving the battle and the 'feeding' of the Harpies.

[]:             Quote Post by EvangelionFan » Fri Mar 30, 2012 6:10 pm Mr. Tines wrote: Nor are we shown one for Asuka, yet she comes back.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Mar 30, 2012 8:06 pm EvangelionFan wrote: So, if we hold that the Angel's individualised forms are determined by their AT-Field and not by genetics, how come the 'Angel Tower' doesn't register as multiple AT-Fields?

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Fri Mar 30, 2012 10:46 pm Kyoko shows up in Asuka's ending in NGE2, so it's likely she could come back in some fashion (whether or not she'd be the same person she was when she underwent her Contact Experiment is a separate matter). As for the rest, technically everyone apart from Yui can come back. This includes Rei/Lilith and Kaworu/Adam, though quantum hijinks mean they probably wouldn't. But anyone else who can face reality is fair game.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Sat Mar 31, 2012 1:26 pm Bagheera wrote: As for the rest, technically everyone apart from Yui can come back. This includes Rei/Lilith and Kaworu/Adam, though quantum hijinks mean they probably wouldn't. But anyone else who can face reality is fair game.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Sat Mar 31, 2012 1:30 pm esselfortium wrote: This already sounds like the funniest planet ever.

[]:             Quote Post by Ceimoa Nan » Tue Apr 03, 2012 12:54 am A couple of you are saying that everyone or nearly everyone can come back; I was asking, assuming that everyone or nearly everyone can come back, who do you think will come back? (Not to sound ungrateful for your replies or anything...) Here are people I'm not sure whether they can come back or not: Rei, Kaworu, Gendo, Kyoko, Kaji. And we know Yui won't come back. Everybody else is fair game. Out of everybody who is fair game, and those of the "not sure" group that you think are fair game, which of them can face reality, and which will continue hiding in the Sea of LCL? In particular, what about Keel, Gendo, Ritsuko, and Aoba? But maybe that's not as interesting a question to others as it is to me. I should look up that thing about Kyoko appearing in Asuka's NGE2 ending, even though I usually assume the only part of NGE2 that's canonical enough is the Classified Information. And Kendrix's first post makes me wonder, after Shinji left Instrumentality, did Instrumentality end for everybody? That's what I assumed, and that everyone who hasn't returned from Instrumentality is in a limbo between Instrumentality and reality. But maybe those who haven't returned to reality are still Instrumentalizing.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Tue Apr 03, 2012 8:08 am Ceimoa Nan wrote: Here are people I'm not sure whether they can come back or not: Rei, Kaworu, Gendo, Kyoko, Kaji.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Tue Apr 03, 2012 1:01 pm Yea, with instrumentality being over, I feel that people re-embodying is pretty inevitable, even if some people drag their asses about it. It's either float in red water forever or be a person and do things.

[]:             Quote Post by chee » Tue Apr 03, 2012 5:11 pm AuraTwilight wrote: Yea, with instrumentality being over, I feel that people re-embodying is pretty inevitable, even if some people drag their asses about it. It's either float in red water forever or be a person and do things.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Wed Apr 04, 2012 9:44 am Ceimoa Nan wrote: Here are people I'm not sure whether they can come back or not: Rei, Kaworu, Gendo, Kyoko, Kaji. And we know Yui won't come back. Everybody else is fair game. Out of everybody who is fair game, and those of the "not sure" group that you think are fair game, which of them can face reality, and which will continue hiding in the Sea of LCL?

[]:             Quote Post by Hawq » Mon Apr 09, 2012 5:43 am Gendo's more likely to be off on a cruise of space with Yui rather than coming back
